claim: Settle commit hygiene for concurrently-written graph repos: a mid-session commit that stages the graph file sweeps foreign in-flight writes and can land a torn HEAD where the repo's own gates are red. Decide and encode the rule - for example, run the repo gates (mix cb.verify.schema, mix cb.generate.claude_md --check) against the staged tree before any commit touching graph-rendered surfaces, or coordinate commits across live sessions.

evidence.detail: First specimen, 585f9ec: a session minting a535/a536 staged beliefs.json while the a531 ceremony was mid-flight in another session, committing the a533/c065 supersessions and t0019 materialization without the matching regenerated CLAUDE.md - at that commit mix cb.generate.claude_md --check fails. No data lost in either direction; the a531 close's commit repairs main. The hazard is structural: CLAUDE.md's own session-start doctrine says other sessions write these repos concurrently, so selective staging of a shared graph file will recur.
